title: Grimaldi Lines — West Africa Express: March 2026 Fleet Rotation Notice
summary: Grimaldi confirms GREAT COTONOU (IMO 9922345) leads the WAX service from Savannah, March sailing. GREAT TEMA listed as backup vessel following engineering hold in Baltimore. Vessel swap possible late voyage.
body: Fleet Rotation Notice — West Africa Express (WAX) Service — March 2026. Grimaldi Lines advises that the WAX service March rotation will be operated primarily by GREAT COTONOU (IMO: 9922345, MMSI: 311000111). The vessel departed Savannah (USSAV) on 16 March 2026 with 11 units of rolling equipment on booking GRI-LAG-24007. Declared ETA at Apapa (NGAPP) is 31 March 2026. Important: GREAT TEMA (IMO: 9919876, MMSI: 311000222) was briefly held at Baltimore for routine engineering inspection. As of 20 March, GREAT TEMA has cleared inspection and is now positioned as backup vessel for this rotation should GREAT COTONOU require substitution late in the voyage. Customers should be aware that a vessel substitution is operationally possible but not currently anticipated. Hull substitutions on the WAX service do not affect cargo routing or ETA commitments unless operationally necessary. Grimaldi will notify customers directly if a substitution is confirmed.

title: GREAT COTONOU — Position Report & ETA Revision (31 March → 2 April 2026)
summary: Grimaldi revises GREAT COTONOU ETA at Apapa from 31 March to 2 April 2026. Revised due to reduced speed through Bight of Benin amid adverse weather conditions and 6-hour unscheduled stop at Cotonou.
body: ETA Revision Notice — GREAT COTONOU — WAX March 2026 Voyage. Grimaldi Lines advises a revision to the declared ETA for GREAT COTONOU at Apapa, Lagos. Previous ETA: 31 March 2026. Revised ETA: 2 April 2026. Reason for revision: (1) The vessel encountered adverse sea conditions in the Bight of Benin on 26–27 March, resulting in a precautionary speed reduction to 10 knots for approximately 18 hours. (2) An unscheduled 6-hour stop at Cotonou anchorage was required for crew provisions resupply. Current vessel position as of 28 March: 4.91°N, 2.44°E, speed 12.8 knots, heading 088°, destination text: TIN CAN. Note: Upon arrival at Lagos, GREAT COTONOU will discharge at Apapa Quays before any Tin Can Island calls. Customers with Apapa discharge should anticipate berth availability 2–4 days after vessel arrival given current port congestion.

title: Grimaldi West Africa Express — Q2 2026 Sailing Calendar
summary: Grimaldi publishes Q2 2026 sailing dates for the West Africa Express service. Four sailings planned April–June from Savannah and Baltimore.
body: West Africa Express — Q2 2026 Sailing Calendar. Grimaldi Lines publishes the following provisional sailing schedule for Q2 2026. All departures are from US East Coast (Savannah/Baltimore rotation) to West Africa (Apapa/Tin Can, Lagos; Tema, Ghana; Cotonou, Benin). Sailing 1: Departure 10 April 2026, ETA Lagos 25 April. Vessel: TBD. Sailing 2: Departure 1 May 2026, ETA Lagos 16 May. Vessel: GREAT TEMA (provisional). Sailing 3: Departure 22 May 2026, ETA Lagos 6 June. Vessel: GREAT COTONOU (provisional). Sailing 4: Departure 12 June 2026, ETA Lagos 27 June. Vessel: TBD. Cargo receipt deadlines are 4 days before each sailing date. Form M and PAAR must be in place before vessel arrival. Grimaldi advises customers on the Nigeria corridor to allow 10–15 days from vessel arrival for complete customs clearance and vehicle collection under current NCS (Nigeria Customs Service) processing times.

title: Grimaldi Group — Nigeria Operations Advisory: Apapa vs Tin Can Discharge
summary: Grimaldi issues updated guidance for Nigeria discharge ports. Customers should note differences in clearance procedures, transit times and agent contacts for Apapa Quays vs Tin Can Island Terminal.
body: Nigeria Operations Advisory — Apapa Quays vs Tin Can Island Terminal. Grimaldi Lines serves two discharge terminals in Lagos, Nigeria: Apapa Quays (NGAPP) and Tin Can Island Terminal (NGLOS). Key differences customers should be aware of: CLEARANCE PROCESS: Apapa Quays — operated under direct NPA supervision. Customs examinations are more frequent (averaging 60% of consignments physically examined). Tin Can Island — private terminal management (Grimaldi's own terminal). Customs examination rate approximately 35%. PRE-ARRIVAL DOCS: Both terminals require: valid Form M, PAAR (Pre-Arrival Assessment Report), Combined Certificate of Value and Origin, Bill of Lading. Documents must be submitted to Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) via NICIS-2 system. Recommended lead time: 10 business days before vessel ETA. AGENT CONTACT FOR NIGERIA: Grimaldi Lagos Agent — Pan Ocean Shipping Ltd, Apapa. Tel: +234-1-XXX-XXXX. PORT CONGESTION: Both terminals currently experiencing elevated congestion. Average berth wait: Apapa 3–5 days, Tin Can 4–7 days. Last updated: 20 March 2026.

title: Grimaldi — GREAT TEMA Cleared for Service After Engineering Inspection
summary: GREAT TEMA (IMO 9919876) receives class clearance following routine engineering hold. Vessel repositioned as standby for WAX service. Ready for deployment April 2026 if required.
body: Vessel Clearance Notice — GREAT TEMA. Grimaldi Lines confirms that GREAT TEMA (IMO: 9919876, MMSI: 311000222) has received full class society clearance following a routine engineering inspection completed on 20 March 2026. The inspection was scheduled maintenance and was not related to any operational incident. The vessel is now repositioned at Baltimore and is available for deployment on the West Africa Express service. GREAT TEMA will serve as backup vessel for the current March WAX rotation, and is confirmed as the primary vessel for the May 2026 WAX sailing (provisional departure 1 May). Grimaldi apologises to customers for any uncertainty caused by the brief inspection hold. No cargo or bookings were affected. VESSEL SPECS — GREAT TEMA: Built 2021, GRT 62,890, ro-ro capacity 4,850 CEU, max speed 19.5 knots.

title: Grimaldi Customer Advisory - Possible Hull Substitution Protocol for WAX Service
summary: Grimaldi outlines how customers will be notified if a WAX service hull substitution becomes operationally necessary. Bookings remain valid and cargo routing does not change, but ETA windows may shift by 24-72 hours.
body: Hull Substitution Protocol Advisory - West Africa Express. Grimaldi Lines advises customers that the WAX service may occasionally require a late operational vessel substitution when engineering readiness, berth sequencing, or intermediate-port timing changes materially affect the published rotation. If a substitution is made, customer bookings remain valid and cargo discharge port remains unchanged unless explicitly restated. WHAT CHANGES FOR CUSTOMERS: The vessel name on the booking confirmation may be updated. AIS tracking should follow the newly assigned hull once Grimaldi confirms the substitution. ETA can shift by 24-72 hours depending on where in the voyage the change occurs. WHAT DOES NOT CHANGE: Freight terms, destination port, cargo booking reference, and local agent point of contact remain unchanged. CURRENT GUIDANCE FOR LATE-MARCH CUSTOMERS: GREAT COTONOU remains the active lead hull for the present WAX rotation, with GREAT TEMA maintained in hot-standby readiness. Customers should monitor direct carrier notices rather than infer a confirmed substitution from third-party tracking alone.

title: Apapa Arrival Planning Note - Downstream Delay Risk After ETA Revision
summary: Following recent ETA revisions into Lagos, Grimaldi advises importers not to lock customs examination, trucking, or warehouse appointments to the raw carrier ETA without berth confirmation.
body: Arrival Planning Note - Lagos Import Deliveries. Grimaldi Lines reminds Nigeria-bound customers that a declared ETA into Lagos is not the same as a berth-confirmed discharge window. Under current March-April congestion conditions at Apapa, the gap between vessel arrival and discharge start can still run 3-5 days even when the vessel reaches Lagos approaches on schedule. CUSTOMER PLANNING GUIDANCE: Do not lock customs broker examination bookings, truck dispatch, or warehouse receiving appointments to the raw arrival date alone. Use berth confirmation or local-agent advice before finalising paid downstream slots. PRACTICAL RULE: If the carrier ETA slips by more than 24 hours, assume downstream appointments may need to shift by 2-4 days once anchorage and inspection effects are included. This advisory is intended to reduce avoidable rebooking costs for importers whose cargo is already correctly documented but operationally early for collection.
